title: Humboltia bivalvis
taxonomy: Humboltia bivalvis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, which is commonly known as the legume family. This classification is due to the plant's characteristics, including its seed pods and leaf structure, which are similar to those of other legumes. The genus Humboldtia is a part of the tribe Millettieae within the subfamily Faboideae. Further classification of Humboltia bivalvis is at the species level, as it is a distinct and separate entity within the larger family. The classification of Humboltia bivalvis is subject to ongoing research and potential revisions in the field of botany.

In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, it is used to treat various health issues, including fever and inflammation. The plant is also used to treat skin conditions and digestive problems. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Humboldtia bivalvis in modern medicine.
